story: At six, I was sure I’d seen a real Brownie dart beneath the upside‑down boat across the snowy beet field. I wrote my Christmas letter and begged Dad to help me leave it under the boat, since I wasn’t allowed to cross the street alone. At dusk we crunched through the snow together, and I slid the note into the shadowy gap. On the way home, Dad squeezed my hand. He’d slip back after I fell asleep to collect the letter and make sure “Santa” got my wishes, maybe even leave a few cookie crumbs behind. I went to bed tingling with the feeling of being watched—in the best way.
implausible_knowledge: He’d slip back after I fell asleep to collect the letter and make sure “Santa” got my wishes, maybe even leave a few cookie crumbs behind.
question: The next morning, before finding out what happened to the letter, what will she do?
answer_with_knowledge: Stay home calmly, already trusting that Dad collected the letter and that her wishes are on their way.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask Dad to walk her back to the boat to check whether the Brownies found the letter.
